birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in remote montane forests of Foja Mountains range, Western New Guinea at an altitude over 1,150 metres

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Originally described in 1895 based on trade skins, this elusive bird remained a mystery for nearly a hundred years, until 31 January 1981 when the American ornithologist Jared Diamond discovered the home ground of the golden-fronted bowerbird at the Foja Mountains in the Papua province of Indonesia."Thought extinct, bird found in New Guinea", Edmonton Journal, 11 November 1981, p
